summary: Frontend UX fix for Teams integration banners verified. All banners now use text-sm (upgraded from text-xs), have proper colored backgrounds, icons, and text wrapping. Teams fallback appointment shows orange 'Mode legacy — identité technique' banner with full text visible and link to /settings/integrations. Meet Gmail personal and Physical appointments show correct behavior (no regression).

context_for_next_testing_agent: UX fix for Teams integration banners verified. All banners upgraded from text-xs to text-sm for better visibility. Key verified elements: (1) Teams fallback appointment shows orange 'Mode legacy — identité technique' banner with full text, AlertTriangle icon, and link to /settings/integrations, (2) Teams account warning shows amber background, (3) Proof availability block shows green 'Récupération automatique' for Teams, (4) Meet Gmail personal shows red 'Pas de récupération automatique' banner, (5) Physical appointments correctly hide all video-related blocks. Note: Outlook upgrade banner (outlook-upgrade-scope-banner) could not be tested because Outlook is NOT connected for the test user - the banner only appears when Outlook IS connected but WITHOUT OnlineMeetings scope.
note_on_outlook_banner: The outlook-upgrade-scope-banner could not be tested because Outlook is NOT connected for the test user. The banner only appears when Outlook IS connected but WITHOUT the OnlineMeetings.ReadWrite scope. Code review confirms the banner is correctly implemented with text-sm, orange background, AlertTriangle icon, and 'Reconnecter Outlook' button.
